title: lichen_l2_enable re-init races with concurrent TX/RX
description:
Location: lichen_l2.c:311-329

**What goes wrong:** When lichen_l2_enable(true) re-initializes link_ctx (lines 317-328), it does so without holding tx_mutex or rx_mutex. A concurrent lichen_l2_send() or lora_rx_callback() could read partially-initialized link_ctx state. The atomic_get(link_ctx_initialized) check at lines 247 and 601 uses acquire semantics, but the re-init path does:
1. Check atomic (false) 
2. Get EUI-64
3. secure_zero()
4. lichen_link_init()
5. atomic_set(true)

Between steps 3 and 5, a concurrent path could see initialized=false, wait, then retry and see initialized=true but read partially-constructed link_ctx.

**Fix:** Acquire both mutexes before re-initializing link_ctx in the enable(true) path, mirroring the cleanup in enable(false).
